fields.definition_origin: Synthesis item — combines Daniel Kahneman's Peak-End Rule (1993, 'Memories of Past Pain' + 1996 'When more pain is preferred to less' colonoscopy study) with the four-archetype emotional-ladder framework codified in `.agent/master-prompts/v2/02_emotional_spec.md` (TokenCraft) and the Cal AI / Mau Baron / Headspace 2024 onboarding teardowns. The framing 'onboarding's peak = AI plan reveal; onboarding's end = paywall' was articulated by Eleken (2025), Adapty's High-Performing Paywall 2026 report, and Mau Baron's Starter Story playbook.
fields.core_mechanism: The user's first session is a single discrete experience that the brain stores as a composite of its emotional peak + its end moment, NOT its average. Peak-End Rule (Fredrickson & Kahneman 1993) shows duration is largely ignored when memory is consolidated. Onboarding therefore has two load-bearing surfaces: (a) the engineered PEAK — typically the AI plan reveal at step N-1 with theatrical loader (item 13) cross-stitching identity inputs (item 14) — which fires mesolimbic dopamine at maximum amplitude; (b) the END — typically the paywall at step N or the home-screen empty state, which is the user's last conscious gestalt before the session terminates. If either degrades, retention collapses regardless of how good the middle was. The emotional ladder layers this: curiosity (affirm) → trust (social proof) → investment (personalize) → reveal (peak) → commitment (paywall/end). Each rung must EARN the next; skipping rungs flattens the peak.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Peak-End is sustained by hippocampal binding of emotional-arousal moments (LeDoux 2000) and the amygdala's affect-tagging of session-bookend events. Mesolimbic dopamine (nucleus accumbens) spikes most reliably during anticipation of variable reward (Berridge wanting/liking, Schultz 1997) — which is what the AI Plan Reveal loader engineers. The 'end' moment recruits ventromedial prefrontal cortex evaluation of the just-completed experience.
fields.empirical_evidence: Kahneman 1993 cold-pressor study (N=32): subjects preferred a longer painful trial with a less-painful tail over a shorter trial of pure peak pain — duration ignored. Kahneman 1996 colonoscopy study (N=682): patient ratings correlated 0.67 with peak+end average, 0.06 with duration. NN/g (2022) onboarding studies show users underrate session length when peak emotion was high and end was satisfying. Cal AI (Y1 $30M ARR): peak engineered at step 30 (AI macro plan reveal), end engineered at step 33 (paywall conversion). Mau Baron Prayer Lock: 5min→15min onboarding tripled organic conversion when the personalized prayer reveal (peak) and 'lock in your daily practice' paywall (end) were both upgraded — duration alone didn't drive the lift; the peak-end pair did. Headspace 2024 onboarding teardown: ~70% completion (vs ~40% prior) attributed to a stronger personalized-plan reveal peak + clearer trial paywall end. Adapty 2026 documents 1.35% trial conversion at the onboarding paywall, the highest of any placement — consistent with end being a load-bearing memory anchor.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Day-7 session-recall NPS ('How memorable was your first session?'); D7/D30 retention; paywall_view → trial_started conversion rate; emotional-state survey post-onboarding (3-question PANAS-short pre vs post); 12mo cohort LTV vs control without engineered peak/end.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Mau Baron Prayer Lock: 3× organic conversion lift from peak-end engineering (case study, single-app). Adapty 2026: 1.35% trial conversion at engineered onboarding-paywall end. Headspace 2024: ~75% relative completion lift after peak-end upgrade. NN/g 2022: front-loaded delight without mid-journey peak underperforms retention by 18-24%.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Audit every onboarding screen against the emotional-ladder rung it serves — affirm/trust/investment/reveal/commitment. Forbid screens that serve none. (2) Engineer the PEAK at step N-1: theatrical loader (4-7s) → AI plan reveal stitching ≥3 user inputs verbatim → 70/25/5 confetti tier roll → Reanimated 3 worklet at 60fps minimum. (3) Engineer the END deliberately: paywall is the canonical end for monetized apps (Adapty 1.35% conversion). For non-monetized, the end is the home-screen first interaction with seeded data (item 21). DO NOT let the end be a blank screen, a generic 'Welcome to [App]', or a Stage 04 implementation residue. (4) Cross-step stitching (item 14) compounds peak amplitude — the reveal that echoes user words verbatim feels orders of magnitude more peak than a generic plan card. (5) Mid-onboarding review prompt (item 15) at the engineered peak captures the App Store rating during maximum positive affect. (6) Persist a named INVESTMENT artifact (plan_id, named avatar, streak) at the end so the user has a return surface for Day-1 push.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI: peak at step 30 (macro plan reveal with theatrical loader and confetti); end at step 33 (paywall framed 'Start your trial — keep your plan'); $30M ARR Y1. (2) Prayer Lock (Mau Baron): peak = AI-generated personalized prayer reveal stitching 4 user inputs; end = paywall framed 'Lock in your daily practice'. 3× organic conversion lift. (3) Headspace 2024: peak = personalized plan reveal after 'What brings you here?' chip selection; end = 7-day-trial paywall with annual default; ~70% completion. (4) Calm 2024: peak = personalized sleep-plan reveal after soundscape demo; end = trial paywall. (5) Headway: peak = personalized reading plan stitched with goal+pace inputs; end = annual paywall with 'unlock your reading life' framing. (6) Duolingo: peak = first lesson completion with streak start animation; end = streak-freeze grant (engineered loss-aversion softener) at home screen — the END here is the first home-screen state, not a paywall.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary. Mobile's full-screen attention + haptic-driven peak feedback + lock-screen end-state (Live Activities iOS 16.1+ / Live Updates Android) make peak-end engineering more durable. Web equivalent: peak = email-confirmed plan reveal; end = web-checkout / dashboard first state. Web peak-end is weaker because tabs distract and browser back-button erases end-state.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) translucent material is purpose-built for the reveal screen — depth and refraction amplify the peak's visceral impact. M3 Expressive (Android 16+) shape-morphing celebrations and motion springs deliver the peak with native expressivity (4× faster element discovery per 18k-participant I/O 25 study). Both platforms make peak-end celebrations feel native rather than slop-Lottie-overlay.
fields.category_fit: STRONGEST: wellness/habit (Calm, Headspace, Cal AI), AI-wrapper (Prayer Lock, ChatGPT-onboarding-style), social, fitness, edtech (Headway, Blinkist). ACCEPTABLE: dating (Hinge prompts arc has implicit peak-end), journaling, gaming. WEAK: pure utility (cinematic 4-screen pattern is sufficient), B2B productivity (peak-end framing reads as theatrical for tools that should feel professional). EXCLUDE: fintech compliance flows where KYC dominates; the peak-end is the 'account funded' moment, not an engineered reveal.
fields.tooling_options: Reanimated 3 + react-native-skia for the peak celebration animation; Lottie / Rive for archetype-aligned motion (NOT generic celebration); RevenueCat Paywalls SDK Template 5 for the paywall-as-end; Plotline for theatrical-loader orchestration; OpenAI / Anthropic for the AI Plan Reveal generative endpoint; PostHog for peak-end metric instrumentation; UXCam session replay to verify peak-affect duration in the wild.
fields.ai_dependency: optional: Peak-End engineering works without AI (Duolingo's streak-start animation is the peak; no LLM). However, the 2024-2026 strongest implementations (Cal AI, Prayer Lock, Headspace AI Stories, Calm AI sleep stories) use an LLM at the peak to generate the personalized payoff. When AI is used, frontier-class model required (Claude Sonnet 4.6 / GPT-4o); small models produce generic output that flattens the peak.
fields.claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Audit the onboarding flow as a peak-end memory architecture. Identify the engineered PEAK (step N-1, AI plan reveal with theatrical loader + confetti + cross-step stitching) and the engineered END (step N paywall OR home-screen first interaction). Forbid: peak before user has invested meaningfully (sunk cost not loaded); end that defaults to a blank welcome screen; peak with deterministic confetti (extinguishes the dopamine spike); end without a persisted Investment artifact (plan_id / streak / named avatar).' Prompt fragment 2: 'For each onboarding screen, label which emotional-ladder rung it serves: affirm | trust | investment | reveal (peak) | commitment (end). Forbid screens that serve none. Verify the ladder is monotonic — no rung skipped.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate the engineered PEAK screen for the onboarding flow. Layout: theatrical 5-second loader (full-bleed, cycling copy: "Reading your responses... Crafting your plan... Almost there...") then morph into a personalized plan reveal card. Reveal card: full-bleed, 56pt rounded display headline echoing user identity verbatim ("Your Anchor Practice"), 3 stat cards revealed in stagger (200ms each), brand-color celebration motif unique to archetype (NOT generic Lottie confetti). Forbid Inter + indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the engineered END screen — the paywall. Side-by-side plan cards (post-toggle-ban Apple 3.1.2 effective 2026-01), Annual + 3-day-trial as default "Most Popular", framed as "Lock in your [transformation]" not "Subscribe". Forbid toggle paywalls, fake countdown timers, confirm-shaming close.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically (1) flattens peak by using deterministic confetti (same animation every win = fixed-ratio extinction), (2) lets end default to a generic welcome screen instead of an engineered paywall or seeded-data home, (3) fires the peak BEFORE the user has invested (no sunk cost loaded), (4) skips cross-step stitching at the peak (generic plan card breaks the climax), (5) defaults to indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on the reveal screen (slop signature). Counter-prompt: 'Engineer one explicit PEAK at step N-1 (theatrical loader + cross-step-stitched AI reveal + 70/25/5 randomized confetti tier) and one explicit END (paywall OR seeded-data home). Forbid generic celebration. Verify the peak fires AFTER user has invested ≥4 identity questions. Forbid indigo-500.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Peak before investment loaded — no sunk cost = no climax. (2) End that's a blank welcome screen — last gestalt collapses to nothing. (3) Deterministic peak (same confetti every time) — fixed-ratio reinforcement extinguishes dopamine. (4) Multiple competing peaks (3 reveal moments) — each amplitude smaller than one engineered peak. (5) Peak-end design without archetype lock — generic celebration on a Caregiver-archetype app reads as Jester slop. (6) Toggle paywall as the end (BANNED Apple 3.1.2 2026-01) — the end can't be a regulatory violation. (7) Ignoring duration as a free dimension — Cal-AI / Mau-Baron prove longer onboarding with stronger peak-end OUTPERFORMS shorter onboarding without; don't cap step count to optimize average.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium: Engineered peaks that exploit dopamine variability fall under the EU Digital Fairness Act Q4 2026 draft as 'addictive design'. UK AADC + CA AADC prohibit nudge-design on minors. The engineered END as a hard paywall is fine but must comply with Apple 3.1.2 (no toggle), FTC Click-to-Cancel symmetry, and DFA's confirm-shaming ban. Streak-loss-aversion as the end (Duolingo 2023 'Don't let Duo down' incident) generated regulatory scrutiny — soften loss-framing per Caregiver/Innocent archetypes.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) — data minimization at peak (don't collect peak data beyond declared core function). Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (effective 2026-01) — paywall-as-end must be side-by-side or timeline transparency, never toggle. Google Play target API 35 (Android 15, deadline 2025-08-31). EU DFA Q4 2026 — peak/end audit for dark-pattern alignment.
fields.accessibility_implications: Peak celebration animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion (replace with crossfade — NEVER motion-only state indicator per WCAG 2.2 § 2.3.3). Sound-on celebrations must respect mute. Touch targets at peak/end CTAs ≥ 24×24 px (WCAG 2.2 § 2.5.8). VoiceOver/TalkBack must announce the peak content (the AI-generated plan must be readable by screen readers). Theatrical loader copy must be VoiceOver-accessible (live-region announcement). Color contrast on reveal card ≥ 4.5:1.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Peak amplitude is brand-specific — Caregiver archetypes feel peak as 'gentle aha', Magician as 'dramatic reveal', Jester as 'playful surprise'. Do not generic-Lottie all archetypes. (2) Latency to peak: LLM reveal must complete within theatrical-loader budget (4-7s) — fallback static plan if exceeds. (3) Cost: peak generation at scale ($0.01-0.05/user × 100K MAU = $1-5K/mo) — cache by similar-input profiles. (4) End-state durability: paywall close → home should preserve the engineered peak's investment artifact (plan_id) on screen, not erase it.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Peak-End is universal (cross-cultural Kahneman replications). Cultural expression of peak varies: collectivist markets (CN/JP/KR) respond to peaks framed as 'tribe' rewards (5M people pray daily) more than individual achievements. Western individualist markets respond to peaks framed as personal transformation. RTL (AR) layouts must mirror peak-celebration motion. Latin America responds to expressive emotion at peak (Adapty 2026: aggressive monthly-equivalent paywall framing wins). JP responds to social-proof testimonials embedded in the peak reveal.
fields.prescribed_action: Engineer ONE explicit PEAK at step N-1 (theatrical loader + AI plan reveal cross-stitching ≥3 user identity inputs verbatim + archetype-aligned celebration motion respecting prefers-reduced-motion) and ONE explicit END (paywall side-by-side post-toggle-ban OR seeded-data home with persisted Investment artifact). Audit every screen against the emotional-ladder rung it serves; forbid screens that serve none. Place the mid-onboarding review prompt (item 15) immediately after the peak to capture the App Store rating during maximum positive affect.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: The questionnaire IS the trust+investment ladder rungs. Sequencing (item 26) builds the rungs that earn the peak. Cross-step stitching (item 14) at the peak makes the reveal feel orders of magnitude bigger than a generic plan card.
fields.aha_relevance: The PEAK in peak-end onboarding IS the engineered Aha moment. The Aha is created BY the peak (AI plan reveal at step N-1) — before the user has touched the core feature. This is what allows long onboarding (Cal AI 33 steps) to outperform short onboarding on conversion: Aha is front-loaded into the peak.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Strongest with Magician (transformation arc — AI reveal as the spell, end as the 'lock in') and Hero (the journey crescendos at peak, commitment at end). Compatible with Caregiver (gentle peak, soft end), Lover (relatedness peak via tribe rewards), Sage (wisdom peak via insight reveal). MISMATCH: Outlaw (rebellion archetype reads engineered peak as conformist), Innocent (over-engineered peak feels manipulative). Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Peak_End_Rule_Onboarding.json and .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Wasted Loading Screens (the loader IS the peak — never blank), Generic Celebration (mismatched archetype voice flattens the peak), Decision Paralysis (5+ paywall plans dilute the end), Toggle Paywall (banned 2026-01 — the end cannot be a regulatory violation). Aligns with AI Plan Reveal (item 12), Theatrical Loader (item 13), Cross-Step Personalization Stitching (item 14), Mid-Onboarding Review (item 15), Hard Paywall at Onboarding Climax (item 34).
sources: [Kahneman, Fredrickson, Schreiber, Redelmeier — 'When more pain is preferred to less' (1993)](https://psycnet.apa.org/record/1994-32957-001)
[Kahneman & Redelmeier 1996 — Patients' memories of painful medical treatments (Pain journal)](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/8826492/)
[Eleken — 3 Aha Moment Examples That Will Leave You Forever Hooked](https://www.eleken.co/blog-posts/aha-moment-examples)
